mechanism_narrative: When real activity in retail spending is trending higher, expected corporate revenues rise and cyclical earnings revisions improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions faster than earnings can adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deterioration in household income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.30 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or downside hit rate is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the federal funds rate is rising, markets price a more restrictive policy path that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger has no lower average 20-day return than either single-series trigger or if interaction coefficient is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production indicates improving factory output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO upside crossings are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases or mean triggered return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts signals weaker construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are not associated with lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger periods or if downside hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment rises while unemployment falls, labor income and consumption expectations improve together, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over 3 months and UNRATE declines over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform the unconditional 20-day return or if the interaction term is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth remains positive while retail sales also rise, broad demand strength confirms an earnings expansion regime, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases quarter over quarter and RSAFS increases over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods with only one series growing or if Rank IC is negative.
